title: Privacy Mail Ghost Account with Virgin Card Burst Pattern
narrative: Account registers with ProtonMail or Tutanota domain, then executes 3-6 transactions totaling $800-$1,400 within first 72 hours of card appearance in system. Card shows zero prior TransactionID linkage across any merchant or BIN. Transactions cluster in digital goods (ProductCD='W') or gift card MCCs. Email domain appears for first time in database concurrent with card1's first use. Shipping address (addr1) is either null or shows dist1 > 3000km from P_emaildomain geolocation when determinable. Velocity pattern suggests automated checkout: 90-180 second intervals between transactions, all approved, no step-up auth. Card1_txn_count_1day spikes from 0 to 5+ with no gradual ramp. Pattern exploits privacy-mail legitimacy while card and email are both operationally invisible until burst completes.
indicators: P_emaildomain in privacy-focused providers (ProtonMail, Tutanota, Mailfence) with zero historical transaction count in system
card1 first appearance: card1_txn_count_lifetime = 0 before burst, then 3-6 transactions in 72 hours
Total burst TransactionAmt between $800-$1,400 with ProductCD='W' or MCC 5815/5944
dist1 > 3000km when P_emaildomain geodata available, or dist1 null in 60%+ of burst transactions
Inter-transaction intervals 90-180 seconds, suggesting scripted checkout without human browsing delays
addr1 either null or shows single-use pattern: no other cards linked to same address hash
All transactions approved in initial burst, no declines or CVV/AVS mismatches to trigger fraud review
summary: A brand-new privacy email address (ProtonMail, Tutanota) and a never-before-seen card both appear simultaneously, then execute 3-6 digital goods or gift card purchases worth $800-$1,400 within 72 hours at automated 90-180 second intervals. The shipping address is either missing or shows extreme geographic distance from the email domain, and no other cards or emails share this address.
reasoning: Fraudsters use privacy email services and freshly compromised cards to remain invisible until a rapid burst completes, exploiting the legitimacy of privacy providers while the automated checkout cadence and high-value digital goods indicate they are racing to extract value before the real cardholder notices unauthorized charges.
indicator_explanations: Email comes from privacy-oriented service with no previous purchase history
Brand new card suddenly fires off multiple transactions within three days
Burst totals mid-range amount targeting digital goods or subscription merchants
Geographic indicators either missing or show implausible international distance
Transactions space out mechanically like automated script cycling through checkouts
Billing address appears nowhere else or is completely absent from records
Every transaction sails through approval without triggering standard fraud checks
